questions.question: Read the given sentence carefully. Change the meaning of the sentence by replacing the underlined word with its ANTONYM from the following options.The <u>equivocal</u> tone of the Prime Minister in his speech caused uproar in the chamber.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM for the underlined word in the given sentence.Because the politician made a <u>candid</u> speech, he earned the respect of the voters.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.Would you please <u>support</u> me for once in your life?
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Glory
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.How can you be so <u>joyless</u> on hearing the news?
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word .Trivial

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Defeat’ in the given sentence. The first battle of Panipat was fought on 21 April 1526, where Babur introduced canon warfare and was able to gain victory on Delhi and subjugated sultanate ruler Ibrahim Lodi.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The committee <u>deposed</u> him from his office.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Ria is liked by everyone as she is very <u>amicable</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Beautiful
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.Language <u>interacts</u> with all aspects of human life and society.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.The rocky terrain made the hike difficult and <u>strenuous</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Resistance
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.His opponents viewed him as stubborn, <u>dogmatic</u>,̲ and inflexible.

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.It is no exaggeration to say the Hussain Sagar Lake is an <u>integral</u> element of Hyderabad and Hyderabad’s history.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Shame
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘bold’ in the given sentence.A person can be friendly, timid, clever, or fearless based on the context of the situation.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Prajakta is a very <u>vain</u> girl.
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.A <u>boisterous</u> horse must have a rough bridle.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Ralph’s condition <u>worsened</u> in the hospital.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The organisation’s <u>furtive</u> actions must be taken into notice and dealt with accordingly.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Stingy

Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The <u>dearth</u> of governmental policies on the basic rights and comforts of the impaired population leads to low standard of public life in a country.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Enemy
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the sentence.He laboured very hard but could not yield anything from the <u>barren</u> piece of land.

Read the given sentence carefully. Change the meaning of the sentence by replacing the underlined word with its ANTONYM from the following options.The <u>equivocal</u> tone of the Prime Minister in his speech caused uproar in the chamber.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.My parents have a <u>fertile</u> land in the backyard.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Lethargic
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in brackets to fill in the blank.The firefighter summoned all her _________ (cowardice) and rushed into the burning building.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.She was known for her <u>refined</u> manners.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word.We should <u>desecrate</u> our national flag.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The truth was <u>revealed</u> in the interrogation.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.The <u>upshot</u> of the consultation with the doctor was an operation scheduled for the next week.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Few people turned up to attend the <u>tawdry</u> promo of the new movie.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word given in the bracket to fill in the blank.The State media houses present ______ (vociferous) views compared to private media.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.The plot of the novel is very <u>dull</u>.
Identify the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Lenient
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Adverse
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Anthony Trollope was an <u>industrious</u> and disciplined writer.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Please don’t touch that, it’s <u>flammable</u> .
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the word ‘Contaminate’ from the given sentence.She spent the entire night working non-stop to clean the blood stains from the rug.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Fastidious
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the given sentence.Mr. Stein is a very cold and <u>hard</u> man.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.My most <u>vivid</u> recollection of that summer is the ocean.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Alive
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the underlined word in the following sentence.You should never visit her without her <u>consent</u>.
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Sacred
